steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The question asks which choice from the notes best supports Kline's claim. Supporting evidence must show concrete proof that Ballard's compositions actually incorporate Native musical elements — not just restate the claim.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — According to Kline, composer Louis Ballard, who wrote Desert Trilogy, relies on elements with origins in Native musical traditions.: restates the claim without providing a specific example from the notes. ✗
  • Choice B — Kline posits that Louis Ballard's compositions rely on elements originating within Native musical traditions; Washington Festival Suite affirms this assertion, incorporating a Pueblo log drum.: the Washington Festival Suite incorporates a Pueblo log drum — concrete evidence that a specific composition relies on traditional Native musical elements. This directly affirms Kline's claim. ✓
  • Choice C — Classical composer Louis Ballard, who wrote Washington Festival Suite, was nominated for the Pulitzer Prize in music in 1971.: lacks the specific evidence of Native musical incorporation needed to support the claim. ✗
  • Choice D — Kline claims that Louis Ballard's compositions, one of which was nominated for the Pulitzer Prize, rely on elements originating within Native musical traditions.: restates Kline's claim and adds a Pulitzer Prize detail, but it does not provide evidence that Ballard's compositions use Native musical elements. ✗
That is the correct answer.
